source_of_truth: draft.md contains manuscript prose; this registry is an audit and navigation layer.

objects.description: Simultaneous impact-only observed residual model u_t = B0 epsilon_t + eta_t with unit-variance structural shocks and additive residual noise; VAR lags and dynamic responses are outside the first paper.
For the robust transformed-cumulant route, additive residual noise is independent of structural shocks and Gaussian, so every linear transform of the noise has zero cumulants above order two.
Structural shocks are normalized to unit variance, and robust-DW candidate impacts are restricted to a normalized nonsingular space to fix scale.
Population sign-restricted set built from a covariance factor P_* P_*' = B0 B0' + V and restrictions R(P_* Q) >= 0.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: If V is nonzero, the standard sign-restricted population set is based on B0 B0' + V and generally differs from the no-noise economic sign set.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: B0(D^2 - I)B0' = V, equivalently D^2 - I = B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'}, showing why positive column rescaling generally cannot absorb additive residual noise.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: M27 common-chart reported standard-DW set: sign-admissible normalized candidates whose standardized recovered shocks pass a J-test stack containing the no-noise covariance moment and finite higher-moment independence restrictions.
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md; manuscript/derivations/dw-robust-comparison-diagnostic.md

objects.description: M25 working result: under residual noise, standard DW recovered shocks are not structural shocks. A rich covariance-whitened J-test inversion is generically empty in population, except for structural-coordinate rescaling cases or finite-moment aliases; finite samples can produce falsely small least-rejected regions around pseudo-candidates.
objects.proof_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs audit and early Monte Carlo triage

objects.description: Population and sample J-test inversion for standard covariance-whitened DW candidates: g_infty(Q)'Wg_infty(Q) and T ghat(Q)'What ghat(Q), with asymptotic rejection when the population criterion is bounded away from zero.
objects.verification_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs early MC and audit before draft theorem wording

objects.description: Robust DW-style sign-restricted candidate set that searches over a normalized impact space, profiles diagonal residual-noise variances, uses the off-diagonal residual covariance restriction, and adds mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u rather than imposing no-noise recovered-shock covariance.
objects.verification_status: M24 conditional audit passed for the local normalized Gaussian-noise higher-cumulant route; M0030 added the diagonal-noise off-diagonal covariance restriction to solve the high-noise power problem; M37 conditionally cleared the six-moment post-M0030 estimator for local theorem-level prose under diagonal Gaussian residual noise, with caveats on normalized scale, optional nonnegative profiled-variance screening, pointwise chi2_6 cutoffs, and correlated or non-Gaussian noise fallbacks; refreshed M28 population/repeated-seed validation passed; refreshed M29 finite-sample pass supports the high-noise divergence story under primary chi-square cutoffs and shows truth-bootstrap widening as a calibration-cost audit

objects.description: Moment-equation representation of the robust DW stack: the off-diagonal residual covariance restriction under diagonal noise, third mixed central moments, and fourth mixed cumulants of z(B)=B^{-1}u, with transformed second moments also entering as covariance products inside fourth cumulants.
objects.verification_status: M24 algebra review passed for centered third moments and fourth cumulants with covariance-product subtractions; M0030 added and validated the diagonal-noise covariance anchor; M37 verified that the implemented stack is one off-diagonal covariance moment plus five mixed cumulants and that chi2_6 is only a pointwise guide; refreshed M28 population verification passed; refreshed M29 chi-square-primary finite-sample pass completed, with repeated-sample, oracle, and truth-bootstrap cutoffs retained as audits

objects.description: Working result: under additive Gaussian residual noise, mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u are not shifted by noise; under diagonal residual noise, the off-diagonal residual covariance is also clean after profiling diagonal variances. In the bivariate normalized system, local higher-cumulant identification follows when each structural shock has a nonzero third or fourth cumulant.
objects.proof_status: conditional-local-rank-audit-passed; M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor added; M37 conditionally cleared the post-M0030 estimator as a local normalized bivariate result under diagonal Gaussian residual noise, not a global scale-identification or coverage theorem; refreshed M28 grid validation passed; refreshed M29 chi-square-primary simulation pass completed for draft evidence

objects.description: M27/M0030 diagnostic statement: standard-DW accepted mass outside the robust-DW set warns that no-noise covariance-target precision may be driven by residual-noise misspecification, while robust accepted mass outside standard DW mainly records the information deliberately lost by profiling diagonal noise variances and dropping recovered-shock covariance restrictions.
objects.proof_status: M27 formalization complete; M0030 robust statistic revision complete; refreshed M29 chi-square-primary simulation pass completed for draft evidence

objects.description: M27 overlap metric bundle for the common normalized chart: accepted shares, symmetric Jaccard overlap, directional containment q_{S|R}=mu(S cap R)/mu(S), and warning share d_{S not subset R}=1-q_{S|R}.
Intuitive sign-set geometry is now covered by the top row of the selected M0030 residual-noise grid, which shows sign/covariance accepted sets moving as residual noise increases.
objects.output_source: Selected visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png. A separate covariance-ellipse panel is optional, not required for the first draft.

objects.description: Selected residual-noise story figure showing sign/covariance, standard-DW, and diagonal-noise robust-DW accepted B-plane sets across increasing Gaussian residual noise using pointwise 10 percent finite-sample J-test cutoffs. The lower high-noise stress column V=(0.5,0.5) has standard DW rejecting true B0 while robust DW contains it without accepting the whole chart.
objects.output_source: Selected visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.md, manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png. Earlier exploratory support: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_figure.py and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_comparison.png. M0030 revised the DGP and robust statistic; refreshed M28 validation passed; refreshed M29 pass supports the high-noise truth-rejection story under the same chi-square cutoffs applied researchers would use; repeated/bootstrap cutoffs are audits.

objects.description: Selected grid-pair story comparing sign/covariance, standard DW, and diagonal-noise robust DW accepted B-plane sets. Both grids invert pointwise 10 percent finite-sample J tests in all rows. The residual-noise grid shows standard-DW truth rejection and robust-DW truth inclusion; the non-Gaussianity grid shows robust-DW widening toward the covariance anchor as higher moments weaken.
objects.output_source: Selected visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.md, manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png; manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_nongaussianity_grid_figure.py, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_nongaussianity_grid_figure.md, manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_nongaussianity_grid.png. M0030 revised both grids to use the diagonal-noise robust row with chi2_6 cutoffs. Refreshed M28 validated truth inclusion, boundary sensitivity, repeated seeds, and weak-moment widening at the first gate; the refreshed M29 pass supports the high-noise divergence and weak-moment readings under primary chi-square cutoffs.

objects.description: Monte Carlo summary of true-B coverage-style inclusion, set width, empty-set frequency, standard-DW versus robust-DW overlap, and directional divergence warnings using the M27 metric bundle. The refreshed M29 finite-sample pass uses chi-square, no-noise repeated, oracle scenario truth, and truth-point residual-bootstrap cutoffs. User decision U0026 makes the chi-square rows the primary applied benchmark because these are the standard critical values a researcher would use under the no-noise DW null. The audit cutoffs quantify finite-sample size and calibration cost. The refreshed run uses 120 calibration replications, 60 evaluation replications, 20 truth-bootstrap replications per evaluation sample, and a 41-by-41 grid.
objects.output_source: M29 refreshed output: manuscript/simulations/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.py, manuscript/simulations/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.md, manuscript/simulations/output/m29_calibrated_monte_carlo.json. Under primary chi-square cutoffs, high-noise standard DW includes true B0 in 0.050 of evaluation samples while robust DW includes it in 0.900. Earlier M35 exploratory support: manuscript/simulations/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_triage.py, manuscript/simulations/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_triage.md, manuscript/simulations/output/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_summary.json.

objects.description: M24 adversarial review of the robust DW higher-cumulant derivation. Conditional pass: cumulant-to-moment algebra, second-moment exclusion, normalization caveat, local rank, Gaussian-noise condition, and bias language are recorded. M37 later audited the post-M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor as a separate six-moment estimator gate.

Direct post-M0030 audit of the reported diagonal-noise robust DW estimator. Conditional pass: the off-diagonal covariance anchor is valid in the normalized bivariate chart under diagonal residual-noise covariance; diagonal variances are profiled, with nonnegative profiled-variance inequalities optional but not imposed in the current figures; the higher-cumulant stack is valid under independent Gaussian residual noise; chi2_6 is a pointwise benchmark; correlated Gaussian noise requires the pure higher-cumulant fallback, and non-Gaussian residual noise generally invalidates the transformed-cumulant interpretation unless extra noise-cumulant restrictions are modeled.

Adversarial review of simulation design and interpretation for noisy sign-set bias, standard DW false sharpening, and robust DW comparison. M30 completed the first audit layer for the M35 triage script: conditional pass for exploratory use only; population-grid and final Monte Carlo audits remain.

Refreshed M28 validation gate for the M0030 revised grid pair. Exact population moment diagnostics support robust-DW truth zeros under diagonal Gaussian residual noise, standard-DW divergence under noisy covariance targets, and weak/Gaussian higher-moment widening toward the covariance anchor. Repeated finite-sample seeds support the high-noise visual story at pointwise chi-square cutoffs, while M29 supplies calibrated evidence.

open_audits.question: Does the M25 standard-DW J-test derivation survive audit, especially the rich-stack generic emptying claim, structural-rescaling exception, and finite-moment alias caveats?
